data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: U.S. Fifth Fleet Observation Tower in Bahrain Hit
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they struck a U.S. Fifth Fleet observation tower in Bahrain.

data.title: U.S. Secretary Rubio: We Should Have Struck Iran Earlier
data.description: According to Jin10, U.S. Secretary of State Rubio said, "We should have struck Iran earlier."

data.title: Congo Ebola Cases Near 3,000 After Data Review
data.description:
Ebola infections in the Democratic Republic of Congo have risen to almost 3,000 confirmed cases, with more than 1,250 deaths, after health authorities reconciled provincial and national databases, according to Bloomberg.

Officials also reported dozens of new cases in the virus's epicenter.

data.title: STOCKS | India State Refiners Seek Crude as Middle East Flows Stall
data.description:
State-owned refiners in India are seeking alternative crude supplies as they worry cargoes booked from Saudi Arabia and Iraq may not arrive on time, according to Bloomberg.

The companies are trying to avoid disruptions to feedstock deliveries as Middle East flows are choked.

data.title: Bitcoin Profitability Improves, But Analysts See Risk of Another Pullback
data.description:
Bitcoin market profitability is improving, but on-chain data still do not confirm the start of a new bull market, and the risk of another decline remains. According to ChainCatcher, CryptoQuant data show Bitcoin's Supply in Profit has risen to 57.5%, up from a June 30, 2026 low of 46.2% and close to 60%.

However, analysts said the recovery in profitable supply still needs sustained confirmation. Historical cycles suggest a bear market is only considered over when two conditions are met: the 30-day simple moving average of Long-Term Holder SOPR stays above 1 for several weeks, and Bitcoin's Supply in Profit remains steadily above 64%.

Analysts noted that this cycle has already seen one false breakout. From April 28 to June 1, the LTH-SOPR average stayed above 1 for 35 straight days, while Supply in Profit briefly reached 67%, before the market pulled back again. At present, the 30-day SMA for long-term holders has remained below 1 for more than 50 days, which is still viewed as an important risk signal for the strength of the recovery.

data.title: STOCKS | Goldman Sachs Raises TOPIX 12-Month Target to 4,500 From 4,400
data.description: According to Jin10, Goldman Sachs raised its 12-month target for Japan's TOPIX index to 4,500 points from 4,400 points.

data.title: Apple Reportedly Seeks 20% Cut in iPhone 18 Pro Max OLED Panel Price
data.description: Apple is pressuring upstream suppliers to sharply lower OLED panel prices as rising memory chip costs push up iPhone overall costs. According to Jin10, foreign media reported that Apple has asked panel suppliers to price the OLED panels used in the iPhone 18 Pro Max at about $70, down roughly 20% from the previous model. Industry sources estimated that panels currently supplied by Samsung Display and LG Display cost an average of $66.5, even below Apple's target price.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Dollar Heads for Best Week in a Month as Haven Demand Builds
data.description:
The dollar is heading for its best week in a month as investors return to the traditional haven amid heightened geopolitical tensions, according to Bloomberg.

The move reflects renewed demand for safe-haven assets as markets react to the latest geopolitical backdrop.

data.title: Euronext Considers Private Securities Market to Challenge London
data.description: Euronext NV is considering creating a market for private securities, a move that could intensify competition with London Stock Exchange Group Plc as exchanges seek to give investors access to fast-growing private companies, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: ECB Still More Likely to Hike Than Hold, Simkus Says
data.description:
European Central Bank Governing Council member Gediminas Simkus said the ECB is still more likely to raise borrowing costs than keep them unchanged, according to Bloomberg.

His comments suggest policymakers have not ruled out another rate increase even as they weigh the outlook for inflation and growth.

data.title: Economists Keep Fed Cut Call as Markets Price in Hikes
data.description: Economists still expect the Federal Reserve’s next move to be an interest-rate cut, even as markets price in higher borrowing costs by year-end, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: EU Escalates TikTok Probe Over Teen Privacy Protections
data.description:
The European Union escalated its probe into TikTok under the bloc’s content moderation rulebook, accusing the social network of failing to protect teen users’ safety and privacy, according to Bloomberg.

The case adds to regulatory pressure on the ByteDance-owned app as EU authorities examine whether it has met platform safety obligations.

data.title: Fuchuang Precision Block Trade Totals 314,000 Shares for 54.02 Million Yuan
data.description: On July 24, Fuchuang Precision logged a block trade of 314,000 shares worth 54.0206 million yuan at 172.04 yuan per share, an 8% discount to the market closing price of 187 yuan, accounting for 3.8% of the day's total turnover, according to Jiemian News.

data.title: Bitcoin's July Odds: 13% Chance of $70,000, Prediction Market Data Shows
data.description: Prediction market data puts Bitcoin's probability of reaching $70,000 in July at 13%. According to NS3.AI, the same data shows a 1% probability of reaching $75,000 and a 14% probability of falling to $60,000.

data.title: Indonesia Metal Exports Face Delays on New Rare Earth Checks
data.description: Indonesia’s exports of some metal products are being held up by new checks on rare earths and radioactive elements such as uranium, disrupting supplies from the major commodity producer, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Bitcoin Supply in Profit Rises to 57.5% as CryptoQuant Contributor Flags Recovery Threshold
data.description: Bitcoin supply in profit climbed to 57.5% as of July 22, up from 46.2% on June 30. According to NS3.AI, CryptoQuant contributor thechessONCHAIN said a confirmed recovery would require supply in profit to stay above 64% for a sustained period, along with long-term holder SOPR above 1. The update points to improving market conditions, but the contributor said the recovery has not yet been confirmed.

data.title: Mittal’s Rajasthan Royals Deal Sparks UK Shareholder Feud
data.description:
Billionaire Lakshmi Mittal’s plan to acquire the Rajasthan Royals cricket franchise has triggered a London lawsuit, with minority shareholders alleging they are being forced to surrender shares worth about $50 million for just £1 ($1.33), according to Bloomberg.

The dispute centers on the company that owns the team.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Amazon Integrates Alexa+ With OpenTable, Grubhub, Uber Eats, Amazon Fresh, and Whole Foods
data.description: According to Jin10, Amazon said Alexa+ integrates with OpenTable, Grubhub, Uber Eats, Amazon Fresh, and Whole Foods to enable voice commerce.

data.title: Market Tends | Meituan Tests Traffic-Light Pause Feature for Couriers
data.description: Meituan has initially built a traffic-light pause feature, and it is now in internal testing. According to Jin10, once the feature goes live, the system will time deliveries when couriers encounter a red light, and after data is improved this year, it is expected to be rolled out gradually to cover more than 1 million couriers.

data.title: South East Water Secures £200 Million Liquidity Boost
data.description: South East Water Ltd. struck an agreement for £200 million ($266 million) of new liquidity, which will help the UK utility boost its cash coffers, according to Bloomberg.
